task_text:
Fix `GENERATE(random(...))` for `long double` not deterministic across platforms

In `generators/catch_generators_random.cpp`, the `long double` specialization of `RandomFloatingGenerator` uses `std::uniform_real_distribution<long double>` to sample values, but the C++ standard does not guarantee identical output sequences across standard library implementations (e.g., libstdc++ vs libc++). All other floating-point types use the portable `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution`. Replace the `std::uniform_real_distribution<long double>` in `RandomFloatingGenerator<long double>::PImpl` with `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution<long double>` so that `GENERATE(random(x, y))` for `long double` produces identical sequences on all platforms with the same `--rng-seed`.
solve_notes: Single-line change in `RandomFloatingGenerator<long double>::PImpl`: replaced `std::uniform_real_distribution<long double>` with `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution<long double>`. The header `catch_generators_random.hpp` already includes `catch_uniform_floating_point_distribution.hpp`, so no new includes are needed. The constructor call `dist( a, b )` is compatible with both distribution types. `double` and `float` specialisations already live in the header as templates using `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution`; the `long double` explicit specialisation in the .cpp was the only outlier using the standard library version.

search_sequence.result: Found `std::uniform_real_distribution<long double> dist;` at line 25 inside `RandomFloatingGenerator<long double>::PImpl`
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description named the exact type and file to change

search_sequence.result: `#include <catch2/internal/catch_uniform_floating_point_distribution.hpp>` at line 14; `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution` available without any new includes
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm no new include directive is required in the .cpp

search_sequence.result: `double` and `float` specializations already use `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution<Float> m_dist;` — confirms the replacement type and constructor-argument order
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm the constructor call `dist(a, b)` works with `uniform_floating_point_distribution` identical to `uniform_real_distribution`

key_decisions.decision: Replace only the `dist` field type; do not touch constructor call or any other RNG logic
alternatives: Also add a `long double` specialization to the header template like double/float
Delegate the PImpl entirely to the header template
key_decisions.reasoning: The minimal fix is only a type substitution; the constructor `dist(a, b)` and call `m_pimpl->dist(m_pimpl->rng)` are identical for both distribution types

exploration_log.aha_moment: Seeing that the header template already uses `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution` made the fix obvious: the .cpp explicit specialisation was the only place the wrong type slipped in.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have inspected the header first to see the template definition before reading the .cpp, which would have immediately shown the pattern mismatch.

minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: needed to confirm constructor signature `(FloatType a, FloatType b)` and that operator()(rng) returns FloatType, matching the existing usage

thrash_preventing_defs.name: RandomFloatingGenerator
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows that the header template specialisations already use `Catch::uniform_floating_point_distribution` with the same field name `m_dist`, confirming the replacement is the correct canonical type and that the include is already present via the header

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient is the edited PImpl and the distribution class (to confirm API). thrash_preventing adds the header template body so an agent sees the established pattern and does not add unnecessary includes or restructure the PImpl.

excluded_defs.name: RandomFloatingGenerator<long double>::next
excluded_defs.reason: calls `m_pimpl->dist(m_pimpl->rng)` which is compatible with both distribution types; no change needed

queries.query_text: Random number generator for long double type produces different sequences on different platforms even when the same RNG seed is provided because the standard library distribution is not portable
queries.justification: Domain terms 'random number generator', 'long double', 'different sequences', 'same RNG seed', 'not portable'. No symbol names. Pre-implementation framing of the observable non-determinism bug.

The literal expression `std::uniform_real_distribution<long double>` is the exact text being replaced. Pre-implementation: a developer searching for the outlier standard-library usage would search for this string.
